title: narcissus serotinus var. genuinus
intro: Narcissus serotinus var. genuinus is a variety of wild daffodil, also known as the autumn-flowering Narcissus or Autumn Crocus. It is a perennial plant that grows from corms, producing white or yellow flowers in late summer to early autumn. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural diuretic. In herbal preparations, the corms and leaves of Narcissus serotinus var. genuinus can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions to reap its health benefits, which include relieving symptoms of arthritis, improving circulation, and supporting the immune system.
